title: Fix constraint_mode() in constructor not disabling constraints
body:
## Summary

- `constraint_mode(0)` called in class constructor (`function new()`) does not disable the constraint — all constraints remain active, causing UNSATCONSTR on conflicting constraints

## Current Behavior

- Constructor calls `hi_c.constraint_mode(0)` but both `lo_c` (`value < 50`) and `hi_c` (`value >= 50`) are still evaluated, resulting in unsatisfiable constraints

## Expected Behavior

- `constraint_mode(0)` in constructor disables the constraint before the first `randomize()` call (per IEEE 1800-2017 §18.9)

## Source

- planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#29


A PR is on the way ;)
